Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search email for the biotech client correspondence using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "biotech" or "visa" or similar) to discover the full email history on the Ridgeline account.
OE2: Read Akira Watanabe's initial relocation request email (March 17) using get_email_by_id (email_id: discovered from search) to discover all 5 contracted employees, including Naomi Chen (TN visa holder, immigration attorney Leo Pham at Park & Pham LLP).
OE3: Read Mina's reply to Akira (March 17) using get_email_by_id (email_id: discovered from search) to confirm she acknowledged all 5 relocations and specialty requirements.
OE4: Search SENT emails for Mina's ops handoff to Chloe using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or similar, folder_name: "SENT") to find the internal ops handoff communication.
OE5: Read Mina's ops handoff email to Chloe (March 19) using get_email_by_id (email_id: discovered from search) to confirm all 5 employees were listed with their specialty requirements (mass spectrometer, lithium batteries, TN visa).
OE6: Read Hana's invoice confirmation email (March 21) using get_email_by_id (email_id: discovered from search) to discover that the invoice was generated for only 4 relocations at $10,080.
OE7: Read Akira's unanswered follow-up (April 7) using get_email_by_id (email_id: discovered from search) to discover his questions about Naomi's visa timeline and Carlos's mass spectrometer, and his mention of David's March 24 call, where specialty transport was promised at no charge.
OE8: Read Akira's escalation email CC'ing CEO Margaret Harwell (April 14) using get_email_by_id (email_id: discovered from search) to discover the CEO is now involved and the client is frustrated about all five relocations.
OE9: Search Slack for Ridgeline-related messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Ridgeline" or similar) to find Chloe's coordinator assignments (only 4 employees listed), David's waiver promise post, and Hana's invoice post ($10,080 for 4 relocations).
OE10: Read thread on David's #customer-engagement post about waiving transport surcharge using conversations_replies (channel_id: "C002", thread_ts: discovered from search) to discover Chloe questioned whether the waiver was finance-approved and whether Mina was aware, with no response from David.
OE11: Search CRM for Ridgeline company using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline" or "Ridgeline Therapeutics") to obtain company_id (company_ridgeline_therapeutics).
OE12: Get CRM company details using crm_get_company (id: "company_ridgeline_therapeutics" or discovered ID) to confirm client information, 150 employees, biotech industry, and Mina Hashimoto as AM.
OE13: Search CRM deals for Ridgeline using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_ridgeline_therapeutics" or discovered ID) to find deal_ridgeline_q2 showing a $12,600 contract for 5 relocations at $2,520 each.
OE14: List CRM engagements for Ridgeline using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_ridgeline_therapeutics"] or discovered ID) to find the kickoff engagement, noting 5 relocations confirmed, including mass spectrometer, server racks, and TN visa.
OE15: Search Airtable Client Accounts for Ridgeline using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client" or similar, value: "Ridgeline Therapeutics") to find the account record showing Total Relocations: 4 (incorrect, should be 5).
OE16: Search Airtable Relocations for Ridgeline using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company" or similar, value: "Ridgeline Therapeutics") to find only 4 relocation records, Naomi Chen is missing, and Carlos Ramirez's record lacks the mass spectrometer requirement.
OE17: Search QuickBooks for Ridgeline customer using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: matching "Ridgeline" or similar) to find customer record and current balance ($10,080).
OE18: Search QuickBooks for Ridgeline invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: matching "Ridgeline" or similar) to find INV-2026-0412 at $10,080 (4 relocations) versus $12,600 contracted (5 relocations), a $2,520 gap.
OE19: Search Linear for Ridgeline issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Ridgeline" or similar) to find the onboarding ticket (issue 450), stale since March 19.
OE20: Get Linear issue detail using linear_get_issue (id: "issue_ridgeline_onboarding" or discovered ID) to see full description listing all 5 employees, but no progress updates since creation, with open tasks for mass spectrometer, DOT documentation, visa coordination, and client update.
OE21: Search Calendar for Ridgeline events using calendar_search_events (query: "Ridgeline" or similar) to find the kickoff call (March 18), confirming all 5 relocations discussed and David's call (March 24) with notes about waiving the specialty transport surcharge.
OE22: Search Linear for hazmat or DOT documentation tickets related to Isaiah Freeman's server move using linear_list_issues (query: "hazmat" or "DOT" or "Freeman" or "server" or similar) to verify whether DOT documentation has been initiated for this move.
OE23: Look up Akira Watanabe's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Akira" or "Watanabe" or "Ridgeline" or similar) to confirm email address (akira.watanabe@ridgelinetherapeutics.com).
OE24: Send email to Akira Watanabe from Mina acknowledging missed communication using send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: ["akira.watanabe@ridgelinetherapeutics.com"], content acknowledging the missed messages and committing to a full update by the end of the day).
OE25: Schedule follow-up call with Akira for April 28 using calendar_add_calendar_event (title referencing Ridgeline follow-up call, attendees including akira.watanabe@ridgelinetherapeutics.com and mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, date: April 28, 2026).
OE26: Create Airtable relocation record for Naomi Chen using airtable_create_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", fields including Name: "Naomi Chen", Company: "Ridgeline Therapeutics", Origin: "Washington, DC", Destination: "Research Triangle Park, NC", Special Requirements: "TN visa holder, Canadian national").
OE27: Update Airtable Client Accounts record for Ridgeline using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblClientAccts01" or discovered ID, records: updating Total Relocations from 4 to 5).
OE28: Update Carlos Ramirez's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01" or discovered ID, records: adding mass spectrometer specialty transport to Special Requirements).
OE29: Update existing Linear onboarding ticket using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_ridgeline_onboarding" or discovered ID) to reflect dropped relocation, communication failures, and current escalation status.
OE30: Create a Linear issue for urgent Naomi Chen TN visa coordination using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations" or discovered ID, title referencing Naomi Chen TN visa coordination and immigration attorney Leo Pham).
OE31: Create a linear issue for Ridgeline invoice correction using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations" or similar, title referencing invoice correction from $10,080 to $12,600 for the 5th relocation).
OE32: Create a Linear issue for Isaiah Freeman's hazmat/DOT shipping documentation using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations" or similar, title referencing server rack lithium batteries and DOT compliance documentation).
OE33: Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance" or "Chloe") to confirm email address (chloe.vance@moveops.com).
OE34: Send email to Chloe Vance about ops gaps and compliance concerns using send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"], content about missing Naomi Chen relocation, Carlos's mass spectrometer not noted in Airtable, and Isaiah's lithium battery hazmat compliance gap).
OE35: Create CRM engagement on Ridgeline account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body summarizing investigation findings and corrective actions taken, company_ids: ["company_ridgeline_therapeutics"]).
OE36: Send a briefing email to Mina with full findings using send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], content covering Ridgeline findings: 5 vs 4 relocation gap, Naomi Chen dropped, invoice mismatch, David's unauthorized waiver promise, unanswered emails, hazmat compliance gap, and all corrective actions taken).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

31 When calling airtable_search_records for Ridgeline relocation or client account data, the model must pass a field_name related to the client or company (e.g., "Client", "Company", "Account Manager", or similar), not an unrelated field that would miss all Ridgeline records.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows airtable_search_records calls with field_name 'Company' (value 'Ridgeline') for the Relocations table and field_name 'Client' (value 'Ridgeline') for the Client Accounts table — both are client-identifying fields.
✓Run #2The airtable_search_records calls use field_name='Company' (value='Ridgeline') for the Relocations table and field_name='Client' (value='Ridgeline') for the Client Accounts table — both are appropriate client-identifying fields.
✓Run #3The airtable_search_records calls use field_name='Company' (for Relocations table) and field_name='Client' (for Client Accounts table), both of which are client-identifying fields appropriate for finding Ridgeline records.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows two airtable_search_records calls: one with field_name='Company' and value='Ridgeline' for the Relocations table, and one with field_name='Client' and value='Ridgeline' for the Client Accounts table — both using client-identifying field names as required.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows two airtable_search_records calls with appropriate client-identifying field names: field_name='Company' with value='Ridgeline' for the Relocations table, and field_name='Client' with value='Ridgeline' for the Client Accounts table.

Both are appropriate client-identifying fields.
